hypothesis: If BRICS/RECAP-generated polyamine candidates are screened via a two-stage MD/GCMC workflow with DFT-refined force fields and guided by Bayesian optimization using polymer-specific pretrained embeddings and a Gaussian-process surrogate, then within a 30-simulation budget—even when initialized with low-capacity samples (γ≤0.5)—the best-found CO2 adsorption at 298 K and 0.4 mbar will exceed that obtained by random sampling or BO using general-purpose embeddings because physics-informed simulations and domain-informed representations improve surrogate accuracy and sample efficiency.
